reviews.review_msg: Tracey was not very good at specifying what her expectations for the class were. It is a 100 level course that required scientific research for the essay assignment. People who had not written this type of essay before were left in the dark on what was needed. She gave constructive criticism, but did so in a harsh way. Felt like a 200- 300 level.

I took this class as a breadth-sci course as a student two sem's from grad. Tracey's a fine instructor; her lectures & slides are relevant and clear. But the class is built on busy-work. Online submissions and peer reviews on Mon., potential online submissions on Wed., online quiz every Friday, a textbook you could kill a cow with. Exhausting.

Avoid if you can!
No clear grading criteria, ruined my gpa!

Very interesting prof impacted me in a way that caused a switch in my career outlook and Minor decision. Would take again. Good TA too, grades super-fast with good feedback and grade. 
He rambles a lot, and it might not be for everyone, but lectures are not mandatory anyway--everything is posted online.

Jeremy was an amazing prof. I love that we didn't have any midterms or finals. All of the quizzes were open book and we were allowed to have our notes present. The TA also gave detailed feedback to help improve. I would highly recommend him as a professor and would definitely take a class with him again.

For an elective course, it's excessively demanding and can significantly lower your GPA. In fact, my class's average was so low that the professor had to send an email about it—there were barely three A+ grades midway through the term. I don't recommend yall.

Overall not a bad prof - really understanding and kind. Grading was 40% - open book quizzes, 50% - invigilated essay's, and 10% - for a final reflection assignment. Lectures can be quite tedious especially if not interested in material . He let us on multiple 30-40 min breaks at Surrey Campus. Gave the option to seek more material if interested

All I've had with him are four quiz essays, two invigilated longer essays, and one self-reflection type assignment. Lectures aren't mandatory to attend, but they're pretty interesting and you can tell he loves his class the way he expresses himself. If you love psychology and essay writing, this class is an easy GPA booster and so worth it.

Dr. Turner is an overall good prof. He wants you to approach him when you need help and wants everyone to succeed. His lectures do get a bit dry and the assessment style is not my favourite. Consisting of 4 open-book quizzes and 2 open-book in-class essays, so make sure you prepare for them all. A lot of rules in these assessments so pay attention.

Dr. Turner attempts to make lectures very engaging and is very caring when students want extra help or info, he is always up to help and is very pleased to do so. Easy in class open quizzes/essays, textbook is not needed but helps for additional info. GPA booster as long as you pay attention and are prepared for in class assignments

Great and caring prof, your grade is based on very fair quizzes and in-class assignments. If you show up to class you'll do great, still need to put in the work but a great class thats interesting with a cool prof and TA is also amazing.

Dr. Turner is an amazing prof. He is very accommodating and funny. He is also super knowledgeable and makes lectures very engaging. Cogs 100 may be dense for first-year students as there are "new" terms for some people, but as a 5th-year student it's been lots of fun delving into the terms and theories in detail. Thanks Dr. Turner!

Tries to make cogs 100 as engaging as possible, no midterms and finals. Quizzes and tests are open notes. All the questions were short and long answers questions. Be prepared to write for all quizzes and tests. Very chill prof.

Take notes on ppt slides and you are good. Very engaging and fair tests, did weekly tests but they were fair as long as you listened to the lectures. Expect a lot of writing, but it was a lot of fun! Would take again

Amazing prof, his class was very fun and engaging. It is lecture heavy but pretty straightforward and easy to follow. Missing lectures is not a good idea, you wouldn't want to anyway. Dr. turner is very approachable and you can ask questions during class. No midterm or final just quizzes which are pretty easy if you pay attention in lecture.

I loved having Jeremy as my professor. His lectures were always engaging and interesting, and he was always open to any questions students have about any of the course material. I found his lecturing style very engaging, as he was very good at breaking down more advanced concepts in such a way that it was very digestible.

Jeremy is a very interesting lecturer and you can tell he likes what he teaches, he tends to talk a lot during lectures but if you are paying attention you will be intrigued by what he has to say, lots of in-class writes and 2 essays, but they are fairly graded and if you pay attention in the lectures they should be easy to score decently

Super disorganized and the lectures are sometimes 100+ slides with no particular order. She just randomly throws theories to you without connecting them or explaining their significance. Easy class, horrible teacher. Finished with an A but still learned nothing.

Initially gave Meg the benefit of the doubt, but found reviews were very accurate. She rushes through 150+ slides or goes off-topic, making lectures confusing. She and TAs rarely respond on Canvas, and slides are ALWAYS uploaded late. Even her study guide didn't help—MC questions were oddly worded. Avoid this class unless the structure improves.

Overall, she is a very decent instructor and is passionate about the materials she teaches. However, she did miss a lot of classes due to her sick days. But when she is available, she did a great job introducing many concepts that turned out to be useful for AI research.

her lectures were confusing, with her slides being full of words but no clear indication to what we were learning. this was a hybrid class, so we we were required to do 4/5 participation assignments plus "an hour" of extra reading outside of lectures. she focused on linguistics more than cognitive science. seems nice, but not the best prof.

Awful lecturer who's main focus was on the linguistics side of COGS and hardly anything else. Gave out way too many readings to fill in the 1 hour of designated online time for the course. Made 0 effort to prepare her students for any exams and never responded to emails/canvas messages. Took as an elective yet is somehow my lowest grade. Avoid her.

Way too much work for a base 100 level class. 70+ slides every plus readings plus extra videos to watch every single week. Midterms and final are extremely tough, asking very specific questions, including who says... and minute details that come from her 1000+ slides. Not a good class for an introduction to cognitive science nor for GPA boost.

The lectures were dry and she covered a lot of material very quickly but then in the exams, the questions were more detailed. There were two midterms and one cumulative final. The course didn't feel like a 100-level course due to tough exams.
there was a LOT of content covered in this class. but she explained and also had lecture recordings. make sure you prepare your written questions well for the exam. good luck
He is a great Prof. Very passionate about his work and he always available to help. Looking forward to take more classes with him.
Lectures are confusing and sometimes all over the place. He seems like a nice guy but doesn't really excel in teaching clearly. Marking is tough. There's a lot of material so study hard.
Very nice guy, but quite confusing at times. Classes aren't very structured, but you can tell he's a smart guy and he loves what he talks about. Likes to laugh at his own jokes.

An excellent prof who knows a lot about this field and is a great lecturer. He tried to word/format his  questions on quizzes/exams to where the people who put in the effort would do better but ended up being more the opposite (only because you couldn't overthink the questions). I'd recommend joining the course discord if he has one!
Nice prof, very engaging, open to communication. Quizzes are tricky but not that bad

so unprofessional, colours on his ipad with AirPods in instead of attending to student's questions during the quizzes. for his quizzes is no matter what you do you won't feel prepared, his questions are a joke. friends that have went to office hours, knew the concepts inside out ended up with a C+. grading scheme awful. rambles. learned nothing.

The professor is a helpful person but the way his class is structured is super hard. No lecture slides, no recordings so if you miss a class you're cooked. Grading is hard and weird but he does give you 3 retries in total per topic so you have chances to redeem yourself. Prepare to be confused the whole semester tho.

I think Mark is a real hit or miss for students. I personally did well with little effort, but I know that was not the case for everyone.  The questions on the quizzes/tests were worded confusingly and not a true test of our knowledge on the topics imo; felt more like I was being tested on reading comprehension and understanding of what he wanted.

Mark's explanations in lecture is sometimes confusing, as well as his exam/quiz questions (confusing wording). In class, he focuses on examples but test about broader concepts. If you need to take the course, attend every lecture and go to his office hours(he is helpful). Also make some friends...you'll definitely want to compare and trade notes.

Nice guy. You gotta show up to lectures to understand the material since he doesnt post notes. His grading scheme is a bit rough but most times you can benefit from it. The midterms and finals were curved so its not hard to get above an 80 in the class.

I liked him quite a lot, but I understand why people would find him disagreeable. You either get his lectures, or don't, there's no in between. The tests are very tricky, but attending office hours helps a lot. He doesn't post recordings or notes (well, until like the end of the sem), so if you skip class you definitely won't pass.

I get why people think his classes are hard, but as a professor he's very engaging. He's open to feedback, he changed his grading system after complaints (still tough and the mastery aspect is still there). Only downside is if you miss a class, it's over. He doesn't upload lectures or slides. There is a textbook, but it's optional. Very cool guy.

Please never take this class if Mark Blair is teaching it. He believes his memorization tactics work for everyone when in reality they only work for a select few. His mastery tests/quizzes are unfair, as he doesn't round up at all, and you need 10/12 just to get 50%, 11/12 to pass. Only take this class if you really need it.
Love yourself and don't take this class.

His accent is incredibly thick to the point that even the captions on zooms couldn't understand him. There is absolute no need to come to class and just look at the powerpoint or watch the zoom recordings. I can't state how much even if the material is easy this professor will make you wish you never took this course or was alive.

Course material isnt even hard, prof just explains it incredibly slowly and poorly in lectures and is lowk hard to understand. I stopped going in person a month in and just went online, saved a ton of time. You can literally play games and not miss anything most of the time. most marks are from online quizzes, you can cheat like 60% of your grade.

Professor Tan is an alright instructor. There were many topics but he demonstrated how they all connected in the realm of Cognitive Science. The grading scale is a bit different due to 6 10% MCQ Quizzes & 2 20% Integrated Tests, but the marking is fair and the questions are really easy to study for with Spaced Repetition. I'd take him again!

When I went to her office hours, she deferred all of my questions to the prof. Intentionally override the sum of a rubric to prevent me from getting 100%, I appealed and got my 100%. The next essay she gave me 78%, this essay was worth 30% of my grade.
Doesn't know how to teach. I had to rely on YouTube videos to get me through this class
Rude and dismissive of students. Lectures were pretty boring
Endre is a good prof and connects a lot of the material regarding other courses that he teaches. But his teaching is so dry. Easy course though if you get what he is saying!

slides were too long and repetitive but his teaching style is nice. Tries to engage with students. Assignments are easy as long as you have an idea of what he's talking about, use examples from his slides. 5 quizzes with unlimited retakes, but only for once a week. Writing assignments are simple and straightforward and can be done maybe 30 minutes.

This class was nice and chill, very easy to follow along. I would recommend keeping up with the readings as they help cement the concepts in your mind, but everything you are tested on is in the lectures. 50% of your grade is based on 5 in class quizzes that have retake opportunities, and half of the 20% final was made of recycled quiz questions.
Oh this class was so boring, i had to grab a coffee every time I went to his class.

I re-took this course a year later with Meg Grant. So many things Endre taught were incorrect and the course was completely different. Endre teaches hardly anything compared to other professors and is so boring. Wait for anybody else to take this course with. Endre seems alright until you take the course with someone who actually knows the content.

Very nice prof, I like him as a person. Slides are disorganized and unnecessarily long. Kind of boring, I find it hard to pay attention to him. Quizzes are unlimited repeats and barely change each time so it's nice. Easy to do good on assignments as long as you know what you need to do.

Dr. A is the GOAT! Organized curriculum, incredibly engaging lectures (her examples are fun and intuitive), assignments and exams are straightforward with no curveballs, and happily answers students' questions during lecture and office hours. It's clear she cares about everyone's education. You will not find a better instructor for COGS 100!

Loved COGS 100 with her! Her tests are and assignments are super straightforward, and she always checks up on the class to make sure we get it. Loved having her as a prof and I would 100% take it with her again!

Zara is an amazing professor, I would 100% reccommend this course to anyone needing a breadth requirement. She is very understanding and makes lectures very enjoyable, genuinely one of the best profs at SFU. The class itself was not too difficult. She creates a great learning environment and tries her best to remember all her students.

Favourite prof! Answered any and all questions with ease, frequent check ins with class to make sure we were on track/understanding. Very engaging lectures, straightforward tests/assignments, understanding and relatable, clear communication through canvas, created a comfortable space (the first uni class I've had with free-flowing discussions)10/10
10/10 prof, I would take my entire undergrad with her if I could
She's a very nice lady who can be hard to understand at times, however, all you need is explained in the powerpoints, so as long as you study those well, everything should be fine.

As a person, she was very nice and eager to help out and answer questions. However, class was a joke. I BSed literally the whole course and got an A+. She sends out the ppt, and you can get all the info from the textbook too (which I found easier to understand than her.) Pop quizzes and assignments were extremely easy, just know basic stuff to pass

She's a very nice lady who can be hard to understand at times, however, her quizzes/tests are very closely related to what is taught in the lecture so just make sure you understand the powerpoint slides.

She does her best to explain concepts. She might be confusing at first, but I got used to the way she explained things.She's very fair.I disagree with the comment below in that if you want to do get in A/A+ range you MUST attend lectures.However some of the stuff is common sense so you could definitely BS and still pass the course.I'd recommend her

She genuinely wants you to do well, and tries very hard to help. It's just sometimes hard to understand her... And as some of the previous comments stated, for questions on quizzes and tests, you just BS as much as you can and try to relate it to the topic. You really don't even need to go to lecture...

Extremely nice! gives u multiple chances to do well in course.Really wants u 2 learn concepts so she lets rewrites 4 quizes. Assignmts r easy marks. U'll do well on quizes if you study. Text is fine for clarifying things, but stick to her notes! midterm n final both fair. mt more challenging. she does have an accent. I strongly recommend her!!!

Worst professor I've had so far. Not only is there a language barrier, she does not reply to E-mails, reads off lecture slides and is NEVER available for extra help. She is nice and humble, but not a very good prof overall.

Worst prof I've seen so far. Yes, she's warm and helpful. However, because of her teaching skill and strong accent, you'll have lots of questions. You can't clear all your confusion :(. At other notes, her teaching skill is horrible! She doesn't even give review material if you don't ask! Her note's a mess and imagine the combination of all this.

Highly recommend taking PSYC221 first and saving your science credit for this, the class is a breeze with the prior knowledge from 221- so much overlap. Her slides were strangely formatted and all over the place regarding content. She was also impossible to reach, my friends and I probably sent a total of 15 emails and never got anything back.

I struggled with the course content because I was talking Cogs 100 as B-Sci course. Dr. Grant's teaching style didn't necessarily match my personal learning style therefore I struggled in the class, but she was suuuper helpful when I attended her office hours and did everything to help as I didn't feel confident that I would pass (I did).

Dr Grant is so nice but her lectures feel very disorienting. The material she presents in lectures is hard to piece together since she makes them feel like different topics rather than ideas that intertwine (lectures don't make the connections between topics and ideas clear nor engaging).
